text: Code reviews are an essential part of software development. When developers review each other's code, they can catch bugs early before the code reaches production. This saves time and money because fixing problems later is much harder. Beyond finding errors, code reviews also encourage knowledge sharing. Team members learn from different coding styles and techniques, which helps everyone improve. This process builds a stronger, more skilled team. Ultimately, regular code reviews raise the overall quality of the codebase and create a culture of collaboration and continuous learning. A team that reviews code together develops better software together.
